projectDescription: Sample applications demonstrating how to build multitenant Java/Spring Boot services with per-tenant authentication via Keycloak, schema-based data isolation using Hibernate and Flyway, and full-stack observability via Micrometer and Grafana.
keyFeatures: DNS-based tenant resolution via subdomain routing (e.g., dukes.rock, beans.rock)
Per-tenant OAuth2/OIDC authentication using Keycloak realms
Schema-based data isolation with Hibernate multi-tenancy and per-tenant Flyway migrations
Tenant context propagation via HTTP headers (X-TenantId) through Spring Cloud Gateway
Tenant-aware caching with custom Spring Cache key generator
AI chat service with per-tenant document stores using Spring AI + Chroma
Distributed tracing and metrics with Micrometer, OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Loki, Tempo, and Prometheus
publicApiSurface: GET /instruments — List all instruments for current tenant
GET /instruments/{type} — Get instruments by type (tenant-aware cache)
POST /instruments — Add instrument to current tenant
TenantContextHolder — Thread-local store for current tenant identifier
TenantDetails record — Core tenant metadata (identifier, enabled, schema, issuer)
TenantDetailsService — Load tenant details by identifier or all tenants
TenantFlywayMigrationInitializer — Per-tenant schema Flyway migrations on startup
TenantGatewayFilterFactory — Spring Cloud Gateway filter that propagates tenant to downstream headers and observability context
TenantClientRegistrationRepository — Dynamic OAuth2 client registrations per tenant
TenantContextFilter — Servlet filter that resolves and sets tenant from request header
